eng: remove debug console output for production cleanliness

Remove console.log/warn from templateResolver (fires on every config lookup),
writeBrainFact audit stub, useSubscription provisioning logs,
AdminTestOnboardingPage industry load log, createDefaultWorkflows success log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): 429 rate limit backoff + admin_settings RLS simplification

1. Supabase client now uses custom fetch with automatic retry + exponential
   backoff for 429 responses. Prevents the auth death spiral where rate-limited
   token refresh fires SIGNED_OUT → clears session → retry → 429 again.

2. Simplified admin_settings RLS from has_role() dependency to simple
   user_id = auth.uid(). The has_role() check created a circular dependency
   with user_roles RLS, causing 42501 on INSERT for super_admin users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

eng: fix admin_settings RLS INSERT policy (42501)

Split single FOR ALL policy into separate SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
policies. The combined policy was causing 42501 insufficient_privilege
on INSERT for super_admin users. Migration deployed via API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

eng: reduce console noise in AuthContext, AdminModeContext, useBookings

- Remove console.log debug statements from AuthContext (auto-create, tenant switch)
- Downgrade console.warn to comments for expected session retry scenario
- Silence AdminModeContext console.error for expected RLS/permission failures
- Replace console.error in fire-and-forget trigger-workflow/busy_blocks catches
  with silent catch handlers (main operation already succeeded)
- Remove redundant cancelBooking console.error (onError toast handles user-facing)
- Update 3 tests to match new patterns

Fix SSR crash on category pages: split LeaderboardMap to defer Leaflet import

Leaflet accesses `window` at module evaluation time. The previous pattern
(dynamic(() => Promise.resolve(MapInner))) didn't prevent module-level
evaluation. Splitting into a separate file with dynamic(() => import(...))
properly defers the import to client-side only.

Fix WebAuthn: move challenge store from in-memory to DB for serverless

The in-memory challenge store failed on Vercel because GET (generate options)
and POST (verify) requests can hit different serverless instances. Challenges
are now stored in a PostgreSQL table with 5-minute TTL.

feat(setup): add guided installer script (PUNT-275) (#359)

## Summary
- Add interactive `pnpm setup` command that guides users through
complete PUNT onboarding
- Detects PostgreSQL availability with platform-specific install
instructions (macOS/Linux)
- Automates database creation, `.env` configuration, dependency
installation, Prisma schema push, and admin user creation
- Includes a demo mode path that skips database setup entirely
- Idempotent design: safe to re-run with existing databases, users, and
config files

## Test plan
- [x] Run `pnpm setup` on a fresh clone and verify full setup flow
completes
- [x] Run `pnpm setup` and select demo mode -- verify `.env` is created
with `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE=true`
- [ ] Run `pnpm setup` on an already-configured environment and verify
it handles existing `.env`, databases, and users gracefully
- [ ] Verify password masking works in terminal (characters shown as
`*`)
- [ ] Verify password validation enforces min 12 chars, 1 uppercase, 1
lowercase, 1 number
- [ ] Verify admin user is created with `isSystemAdmin: true` and can
log in
- [ ] Run `pnpm lint` to confirm no lint errors

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Fix crash on trillion+ numbers in expandNumber()

Numbers exceeding billions caused out-of-bounds access on the
digits vector (expandGroup received values > 999). Fall back to
eSpeak's native alignment heuristics for these — eSpeak handles
up to septillion.

commit.message:
refactor: unify animated routing to meme-score strategy, remove 4.5s hardcoded threshold

The apple_compat branch was using an outdated duration+resolution heuristic
(4.5s / 1280px) to decide HEVC vs GIF, while the non-compat branch already
used the meme-score multi-dimensional strategy. Both branches now use the
same meme-score pipeline:

- meme-score says keep → GIF (apple_compat: playable GIF; non-compat: keep original)
- meme-score says convert → HEVC MP4

Changes:
- img_hevc/src/main.rs:
  - Remove convert_to_gif_apple_compat_check_quality() wrapper and its import
  - Remove MIN_DURATION / is_high_quality branch in apple_compat route
  - Compute meme_keep once before the apple_compat/non-compat split
  - apple_compat: meme_keep→GIF, !meme_keep→HEVC MP4
- vid_hevc/src/animated_image.rs:
  - Remove both internal MIN_DURATION short-animation skip blocks from
    convert_to_hevc_mp4_matched() and convert_to_gif_apple_compat()
  - These were redundant with meme-score (duration is a scoring dimension)
    and actively harmful in apple_compat mode (caused non-playable originals
    to be copied back instead of producing a playable output)

commit.message:
feat(btc): momentum-based direction model replaces lagging indicators

Old model: scored VWAP, RSI, MACD, Heiken Ashi — all lagging indicators.
Result: 31% direction accuracy (worse than coin flip). Higher confidence
= worse results.

New model (engines/momentum.js) uses leading signals:
1. BTC spot momentum 15s (weight 3) — what BTC is doing RIGHT NOW
2. BTC spot momentum 60s (weight 2) — confirms it's not noise
3. Polymarket price momentum 30s (weight 3) — follow the money
4. Polymarket price level (weight 2) — >60¢ = market is confident
5. Settlement trend last 5 (weight 1) — light trend persistence

Old model kept for comparison (oldModelUp/oldModelDown in UI status).
Trades wiped, $500 clean start.

Keep top N most-connected types instead of dropping to modules-only

nodes, rank by edge degree and keep the top N rather than falling back
to modules/targets only. Fixes explore showing only module blobs with
no access or target info.

commit.message:
feat: add Library with persistence, catalog agent, and intel management

Database (Prisma + PostgreSQL):
- prisma/schema.prisma: Analysis + IntelItem models with indexes
- src/lib/prisma.ts: PrismaClient singleton for Next.js
- railway.toml: add prisma generate + migrate deploy to build

Catalog Agent (07):
- src/agents/07-catalog/index.ts: classifies analyst insights by
  impact_area (valuation/management/business), sector, severity, summary
  using claude-haiku (cheapest model, fires async)

API routes:
- POST/GET /api/analyses: save completed pipeline, list with filters
- GET /api/analyses/[id]: single analysis with intel items
- POST /api/analyses/[id]/intel: add intel item + async catalog

fix: slot ID HMAC delimiter collision with time colon

The slot encoder used ":" as delimiter, but time values like "09:00"
also contain ":", causing decode to split into 5 parts instead of 4
and always return null ("Invalid time slot"). Changed to "|" delimiter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prevent co-expression OOM on large studies (#12005)

* Prevent co-expression OOM on large studies with external group by

The rankCorr() aggregate function maintains one accumulator state per
gene group (~20K simultaneous states), each holding all sample pairs.
For large studies like pancan_pdmr_2025 (6,273 samples × 20K genes),
this exceeds available memory and crashes ClickHouse.

Add SETTINGS max_bytes_before_external_group_by = 2000000000 to spill
aggregation state to disk when it exceeds 2GB. This prevents OOM while
adding minimal overhead for smaller studies (~1.2x slowdown vs 1x).

Tested across multiple study sizes:
- chol_tcga (37 samples): 0.8s → 0.8s (no change)
- brca_tcga (1,101 samples): 15s → 19s (1.2x)
- pancan_pdmr_2025 (6,273 samples): OOM → 85s (fixed)
- aml_target_gdc (2,614 samples, 39K genes): OOM → 71s (fixed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix e2e tests for null-correlation genes in co-expression response

Update co-expression e2e tests to account for genes with null
spearmansCorrelation and pValue (uncorrelatable genes: constant
expression, too few samples, or low variance). These genes are now
included in the response with null values.

- Filter correlated vs null-correlation genes before threshold assertions
- Add assertions verifying null-correlation genes have null pValue
- Remove sort-order assertion (endpoint does not guarantee order)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update src/main/resources/mappers/clickhouse/coexpression/ClickhouseCoExpressionMapper.xml

Add Polars/Parquet I/O, fullscreen plot toggle, citation layout fixes

- Convert ICOS downloads and local uploads to Parquet on first load
  (via Polars); LRU cache now stores parquet paths instead of full
  DataFrames, saving ~40 MB RAM for 20 cached files
- ts_plot() uses pl.scan_parquet().select([dt, val, qc]) per series
  for efficient column-pruning reads; fallback to full DataFrame for
  uploads before parquet is written
- Add pyarrow dependency (required by polars .to_pandas())
- Add fullscreen toggle button (⛶) on plot; CSS fixed overlay at
  100vw×100vh with z-index 9999; Plotly resizes via relayout; Escape
  key also exits fullscreen
- Fullscreen: JS computes proportional margin.b so citation annotation
  at y=-0.24 stays visible at any figure height; restores original
  margin on exit
- Fix citation annotation clipping with one file: move y from -0.28
  to -0.24, increase per-line margin from 14 to 18 px

commit.message:
Remove recalculate_trader_stats from fetch-trades to fix timeout

The full-table GROUP BY in recalculate_trader_stats now exceeds the
PostgreSQL statement timeout as the trades table has grown. This added
8+ seconds of dead wait, pushing total execution over Supabase's
60-second edge function limit and causing every workflow run to fail.

Individual trader stats are still updated per-trade via the
update_wallet_activity RPC during processing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: initial PostPilot AI project setup

AI SNS post generator for Japanese businesses.
- Top page with hero, platforms, scenarios, pricing
- Generate page with platform/scenario/industry/tone selection
- Claude API integration for 3-pattern post generation
- 75 SEO landing pages (5 platforms x 15 scenarios)
- Scenarios listing page
- OGP image generation
- AI public channels (llms.txt, agent.json, robots.txt)
- Sitemap with all 78+ URLs
- Architecture documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-derive wardrobe_vibe from quote generation, zero extra cost

Extends the existing GPT quotes call to also return a wardrobe_vibe
style guide in the same JSON response. No extra LLM call, no user input.

The wardrobe_vibe (e.g. "Casual urban - slim jeans, fitted tee, white
sneakers") is derived from the person's traits/appearance and saved to
the DB. generate-preview and generate-remaining already pass it through
to generateVisualPrompt, so the image LLM now invents scene-coherent
outfits that match the persona's authentic style instead of clashing
with the scene.

commit.message:
feat: implement Iris embedding integration with LRU cache

Add embedding provider abstraction and Iris HTTP client:
- Provider interface with Embed/EmbedBatch methods
- IrisClient for HTTP communication with Iris /embeddings endpoint
- CachedProvider with SHA-256 keyed LRU cache
- Batch optimization to minimize API calls
- 14 tests including mock server integration tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(crm): restructure channel_in into channel + method fields

Separates platform (channel) from interaction type (method) for better
analytics. Now tracks:
- channel: instagram, whatsapp, linkedin, email, website, ads, youtube
- method: dm, comment, story, form, scraper, referral, import, automation

The tool used (gaio, manychat, axiom) goes in source_detail.tool.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add light/dark mode toggle with CSS custom properties

- Install next-themes for SSR-safe theme persistence via localStorage
- Define 16 semantic color tokens as CSS custom properties in globals.css
  with dark defaults (:root) and html.light overrides
- Wrap app in ThemeProvider (attribute="class", defaultTheme="dark");
  add suppressHydrationWarning to <html>
- Add sun/moon toggle to UserMenu avatar dropdown using useTheme hook;
  mounted guard prevents SSR hydration mismatch
- Replace all hardcoded hex values across 20 files with var(--color-*)
  arbitrary Tailwind values; nav bg opacity baked into --color-nav-bg var
- Aurora gradient opacity controlled via --aurora-opacity CSS var
  (0.18 dark → 0.07 light)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: storage usage API fallback to Supabase Storage metadata for historical tasks

Pre-recorded total_bytes only exists for new uploads. For older tasks,
recursively query Storage list() API to get actual file sizes from metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
